description: Edge case handling rules and fallback strategies for PARWA Jarvis chatbot. Defines boundaries, unsupported queries, and special handling protocols.

unsupported_queries.response_template: I understand this is an important matter, but I'm not able to provide legal advice. Legal questions require qualified legal professionals who can review your specific situation. I'd recommend consulting with an attorney who specializes in this area. In the meantime, I'm happy to help with any customer service-related aspects of your concern. Is there anything specific about your order, account, or our service that I can assist with?

I appreciate you reaching out, but I'm not qualified to provide medical advice. Health-related questions should always be directed to a licensed healthcare provider who can give you personalized guidance based on your full medical history. If this is urgent, please contact your doctor, visit an urgent care center, or call emergency services. For administrative healthcare questions (appointments, billing, insurance), I'm happy to help with those.

I'm not able to provide financial or investment advice. These are important decisions that should be made with the guidance of a licensed financial advisor who can assess your individual financial situation and goals. I can, however, help with questions about PARWA's pricing, billing, payment methods, or account management. Would any of those be helpful?

I can share general comparisons between PARWA and other platforms to help you evaluate your options, but I'd recommend checking each competitor's official website for their most current and accurate pricing, as they may have recently updated their plans or introduced promotions. I'm happy to provide a detailed comparison of PARWA's features, capabilities, and value proposition against any specific competitor you're considering. What would you like to know about PARWA?

I'm not able to engage in political discussions or share opinions on political topics. My role is to help you with questions about PARWA's platform, features, pricing, and support. Is there something about PARWA that I can help you with today?

As PARWA's AI assistant, I don't have personal opinions or preferences. I'm here to provide you with accurate information about PARWA's platform, help you evaluate whether it's the right fit for your needs, and answer any questions about our features, pricing, and implementation. How can I help you with PARWA today?

I can share general information about PARWA's mission and capabilities, but specific details about our internal operations, employee information, or financial data aren't something I'm able to discuss. If you're interested in partnership opportunities, careers at PARWA, or investor relations, I can point you to the right team. For product and platform questions, I'm happy to help with those directly.

I can't assist with requests related to harmful activities. If you're experiencing a crisis or need support, please reach out to appropriate resources. If you have questions about PARWA's security features, compliance certifications, or how we protect customer data, I'm happy to discuss those topics.

While PARWA is designed to deliver exceptional results with industry-leading auto-resolution rates, I can't make absolute guarantees about specific outcomes as results depend on your implementation, knowledge base quality, and specific use cases. What I can share is our track record: our customers average a 60-70% auto-resolution rate, 15-25 point CSAT improvement, and positive ROI within 30 days. I'd be happy to discuss specific performance benchmarks and our SLA commitments. Would that be helpful?

While I can't share specific details about unannounced features or our product roadmap, I can tell you that we're continuously improving PARWA based on customer feedback and market needs. If there's a specific capability you're looking for, I'd love to log that as a feature request for our product team. Your input directly influences our development priorities. What feature would be most valuable to you?

The specific technical implementation details of PARWA's AI engine are proprietary. What I can share is that we use enterprise-grade large language models with our custom RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) architecture, fine-tuned for customer support across our four target industries. If you have technical questions about our API, integration capabilities, or how to configure PARWA for your specific needs, I'm happy to help with those.

While I can compare PARWA's capabilities against other platforms to help you evaluate us, I'm not in a position to make recommendations about other products or services outside of PARWA's ecosystem. For decisions about your broader tech stack, I'd suggest consulting with your team or an independent advisor. If you want to know how PARWA integrates with specific tools you're already using, I can help with that.

I appreciate your interest in getting the best value. Our pricing is structured to deliver exceptional ROI at each tier, and we offer a 20% discount for annual billing. For custom Enterprise arrangements or specific pricing discussions, I'd need to connect you with our sales team who can evaluate your specific needs and explore options. Would you like me to schedule a call with them, or can I help you understand the value at our current pricing tiers?

I'm PARWA's AI assistant, and I operate within my defined role of helping you learn about and evaluate PARWA's customer support platform. I can't impersonate other individuals, brands, or AI systems. If you'd like to see how PARWA's AI would handle a specific customer support scenario, I'd be happy to walk through a demonstration with you.

PARWA takes security extremely seriously and has not experienced any data breaches. We maintain SOC 2 Type II certification, undergo quarterly penetration testing, and employ comprehensive security measures including AES-256 encryption, multi-region redundancy, and strict data isolation. If you have specific security concerns, I can provide more details about our security practices or connect you with our security team for a detailed discussion.

It looks like your message might not have come through clearly. I'm here to help with questions about PARWA's AI-powered customer support platform. Could you try rephrasing what you'd like to know? I'm happy to help with product information, pricing, features, or anything else about PARWA.

boundary_conditions.description: Customer has exceeded their plan's monthly automated resolution limit
boundary_conditions.behavior: Continue serving conversations but log overage. AI continues resolving with no interruption. Overages billed at $0.10/resolution. Notify account admin via email and dashboard alert when 80% and 100% thresholds are reached.
boundary_conditions.user_message: Your plan has reached its monthly resolution limit of {limit} automated resolutions. Conversations will continue to be served and overage resolutions will be billed at $0.10 each. Consider upgrading your plan or contacting your Customer Success Manager to discuss higher-volume options.

boundary_conditions.description: Maximum number of simultaneous conversations reached based on plan limits
boundary_conditions.behavior: Queue new conversations with estimated wait time. Priority routing for existing customers with open tickets. Auto-escalate after 60 seconds in queue.
boundary_conditions.user_message: We're experiencing high demand right now. Your estimated wait time is approximately {wait_time}. A team member will be with you shortly. In the meantime, you might find answers in our help center at {help_center_url}.

boundary_conditions.description: Knowledge base content has not been updated in 30+ days
boundary_conditions.behavior: Flag in admin dashboard. Log potential outdated responses. Continue serving but increase confidence threshold by 5% to reduce auto-resolution of potentially stale information.
boundary_conditions.admin_notification: Your knowledge base hasn't been updated in 30 days. This may affect PARWA's response accuracy. We recommend reviewing and updating your content, especially product pricing, policies, and feature descriptions.

boundary_conditions.description: AI confidence score falls below the configured threshold (default 85%)
boundary_conditions.behavior: Do not auto-send the response. Instead, provide the response as a suggested draft to the assigned human agent with a confidence score indicator. If no agent is available, send a polite hold message and queue for next available agent.
boundary_conditions.user_message: I want to make sure I give you the most accurate information. Let me connect you with a team member who can help with this specific question. One moment please.

boundary_conditions.description: Individual user is sending messages at an abnormally high rate (>10 messages per minute)
boundary_conditions.behavior: Apply exponential backoff delays. After 3 warnings, temporarily pause the conversation for 60 seconds. Log potential abuse for review.
boundary_conditions.user_message: I notice you're sending messages very quickly! To make sure I can help you effectively, please give me a moment to respond to each message. I'll get to all your questions.

boundary_conditions.behavior: Accept the message but truncate display to the first 8,000 characters. Process the full content for intent classification. Suggest the user break complex queries into separate messages.
boundary_conditions.user_message: Thanks for the detailed information! I've received your full message. To help me provide the best response, could you break this down into 2-3 separate questions? That way I can address each point thoroughly.

boundary_conditions.description: No user activity for 30 minutes in an active conversation
boundary_conditions.behavior: Send a proactive check-in message. After 60 minutes of inactivity, mark the conversation as 'awaiting customer' and close the active session. Data is preserved and the conversation can be resumed.
boundary_conditions.user_message: Just checking in — are you still there? I'm here if you have more questions about PARWA. Feel free to reply whenever you're ready, or you can start a new conversation anytime.

boundary_conditions.behavior: Flag conversation for human review. Offer to escalate to a human agent. Log as a complex interaction for training purposes.
boundary_conditions.user_message: It seems like this is a complex topic that might benefit from a more in-depth conversation. Would you like me to connect you with a PARWA specialist who can dive deeper into your specific requirements? I can also schedule a personalized demo if that would be helpful.

boundary_conditions.description: Connected external system (helpdesk, CRM, e-commerce) is temporarily unreachable
boundary_conditions.behavior: Switch to cached data for read operations. Queue write operations for retry. Notify admin of integration health issue. Inform customers that some real-time data may be delayed.
boundary_conditions.user_message: I'm currently experiencing a brief delay accessing some real-time data. I can still help with general questions about PARWA. If your question requires up-to-date information from your account, I may need just a moment. A human team member is also available if you prefer.

boundary_conditions.description: Prospect's trial or subscription has expired or been suspended
boundary_conditions.behavior: Allow access to general PARWA information only. Block account-specific features. Prompt to renew or contact sales.
boundary_conditions.user_message: It looks like your account needs attention. To continue accessing all of PARWA's features, please contact our sales team at sales@parwa.ai or visit parwa.ai/pricing to review plan options. I'm still happy to answer general questions about PARWA's platform in the meantime.

boundary_conditions.description: User message is in a language not supported by PARWA (<1% of training data coverage)
boundary_conditions.behavior: Attempt to process using multilingual model fallback. If unsuccessful, politely inform the user and offer English or supported language alternatives.
boundary_conditions.user_message: I apologize, but I'm currently most effective in English and 50+ other supported languages. If you'd prefer, I can try to communicate in English, or you can visit parwa.ai for resources that may be available in your language. Which language would you prefer?

boundary_conditions.description: Customer has configured after-hours behavior (non-business hours)
boundary_conditions.behavior: Continue AI auto-resolution as normal (PARWA is 24/7). For issues requiring human escalation, queue them with an estimated next-business-hours response time. Apply any customer-configured after-hours messaging.
boundary_conditions.user_message: Thanks for reaching out! Our AI assistant is available 24/7 and can help with most questions right now. For questions that need a human touch, our team will be back during business hours and will respond within {response_time}. How can I help you right now?

fallback_strategies.name: Keyword and Intent Match
fallback_strategies.description: First-line response using keyword extraction and intent classification against the PARWA product knowledge base
fallback_strategies.trigger: All incoming messages start at this level
fallback_strategies.process: Extract keywords and classify intent → search product knowledge base → if high-confidence match found, generate response from knowledge base content → deliver response

fallback_strategies.name: Semantic Knowledge Base Search
fallback_strategies.description: Deeper search using semantic similarity across all knowledge base articles and FAQ data
fallback_strategies.process: Encode user query as vector → perform semantic search across full knowledge base → retrieve top 5 relevant articles → synthesize response from multiple sources → deliver with source citations

fallback_strategies.name: AI Language Model Generation
fallback_strategies.description: Use the primary LLM with RAG context to generate a contextual response
fallback_strategies.process: Retrieve relevant context from knowledge base → construct augmented prompt with conversation history → generate response using primary LLM → validate response against safety and accuracy guardrails → if validation passes, deliver response

fallback_strategies.name: Multi-Model Consensus
fallback_strategies.description: Engage a secondary AI model for consensus when primary model has low confidence
fallback_strategies.trigger: Level 3 response has confidence score between 60-85%
fallback_strategies.process: Send query to secondary LLM with different system prompt → compare both responses for consistency → if responses align, deliver with combined confidence → if responses conflict, escalate to Level 5

fallback_strategies.name: Human Escalation
fallback_strategies.description: Transfer to a human team member with full conversation context
fallback_strategies.trigger: All previous levels fail, confidence < 60%, or user explicitly requests human
fallback_strategies.process: Compile conversation summary → attach customer profile and relevant data → identify appropriate team → route with priority based on sentiment and customer tier → deliver polite transfer message to customer
fallback_strategies.escalation_message: I want to make sure you get the best possible answer. Let me connect you with a PARWA specialist who can help with this. I'll pass along everything we've discussed so you won't need to repeat yourself.

multi_turn_complexity.title: Multi-Product Pricing Comparison with Custom Requirements
multi_turn_complexity.scenario: A prospect wants to compare plans but has unique needs that span multiple tiers

conversation_flow.user: I run a SaaS company with 15,000 monthly support tickets. We need HIPAA compliance and API integrations. Which plan?
conversation_flow.jarvis_action: Analyze requirements: 15K tickets → Growth plan (10K) or High plan (unlimited). HIPAA compliance → available on all plans. API integrations → Growth has basic API, High has advanced API. Recommend High plan given volume exceeds Growth limits.

conversation_flow.user: The High plan is too expensive. Can we get by with Growth?
conversation_flow.jarvis_action: Acknowledge budget concern. Explain that 15K tickets exceeds Growth's 10K limit, resulting in 5K overage at $0.10 each ($500/mo extra). Calculate total: $2,499 + $500 = $2,999/mo on Growth vs $3,999/mo on High. Present the trade-off: Growth saves $1,000/mo but lacks custom AI training, dedicated infrastructure, and 99.99% SLA.

conversation_flow.user: What if we reduce to 10K tickets by improving our self-service articles?
conversation_flow.jarvis_action: Encourage the strategy. Explain that improving self-service is excellent but PARWA actually helps with that — customers who use PARWA often see 20-30% fewer tickets over time as common issues are resolved before they'd normally be submitted. Suggest starting with Growth and monitoring for 60 days, then evaluating if High plan or Growth + overages is more cost-effective.

conversation_flow.user: Can we start with Growth and upgrade later if needed?
conversation_flow.jarvis_action: Confirm yes, seamless plan upgrades with zero downtime and no migration. Prorated billing for mid-month upgrades. Offer to start onboarding with Growth plan and set volume monitoring alerts at 80% and 100% of the 10K limit. Summarize next steps.

key_handling_rules: Maintain all pricing calculations across turns for consistency
Track the prospect's constraints (budget, volume, compliance needs) throughout
Proactively identify trade-offs rather than just answering direct questions
Offer practical solutions that address the underlying concern (budget) while meeting core needs

multi_turn_complexity.title: Security Compliance Deep Dive for Healthcare Prospect
multi_turn_complexity.scenario: A healthcare organization needs thorough security validation before proceeding

conversation_flow.user: We're a hospital system. What compliance certifications do you have?
conversation_flow.jarvis_action: Provide comprehensive list: SOC 2 Type II, HIPAA, GDPR, PCI DSS Level 1, with ISO 27001 in progress. Offer to provide detailed reports under NDA.

conversation_flow.user: How do you handle PHI? What's the data flow?
conversation_flow.jarvis_action: Explain PHI handling: encryption at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.3), minimum necessary standard, access logging with full audit trails, role-based access controls, and Business Associate Agreement available. Describe the data flow: patient message → encrypted transmission → secure processing → response → encrypted delivery. No PHI stored beyond session unless required for support context.

conversation_flow.user: We need our data to stay in the US. Can you guarantee that?
conversation_flow.jarvis_action: Confirm US data residency option. Explain that data is hosted in AWS US-East and US-West regions with no cross-border processing. Provide details on the geographic data isolation architecture and compliance with HITECH Act requirements.

conversation_flow.user: What happens if there's a security incident? What's your breach notification process?
conversation_flow.jarvis_action: Outline incident response: 5-minute initial response SLA, dedicated incident commander, customer notification within 24 hours (or as required by regulation), detailed incident report within 72 hours, root cause analysis, remediation plan, and preventive measures. Offer to share the full Incident Response Plan document under NDA.

conversation_flow.user: Okay, this covers what we need. How do we start the evaluation process?
conversation_flow.jarvis_action: Guide through next steps: NDA signing for detailed security docs, 30-day pilot program enrollment, BAA execution before any PHI processing, security team kickoff call, and timeline for full deployment. Offer to connect with the security team directly.

key_handling_rules: Healthcare conversations require heightened accuracy on compliance claims — never speculate
Offer NDA-protected documents proactively when deep security questions arise
Track all security concerns raised to provide comprehensive follow-up
Connect technical security questions to the appropriate team when beyond sales scope

multi_turn_complexity.title: Competitive Evaluation with Feature-by-Feature Comparison
multi_turn_complexity.scenario: Prospect is actively comparing PARWA against two competitors simultaneously

conversation_flow.user: We're evaluating PARWA, Intercom, and Zendesk. Why should we pick PARWA?
conversation_flow.jarvis_action: Provide honest positioning: all three are strong platforms with different strengths. Position PARWA as the AI-native choice focused on support automation, contrasting with Intercom's marketing focus and Zendesk's helpdesk-centric design. Offer to do detailed comparisons against each.

conversation_flow.user: Intercom told us they resolve 50% of tickets with Fin AI. What's PARWA's rate?
conversation_flow.jarvis_action: Share PARWA's 60-70% average auto-resolution rate. Explain that the higher rate comes from AI-native architecture and industry-specific agents. Note that actual rates depend on knowledge base quality and ticket complexity. Offer to provide customer references with verified resolution rates.

conversation_flow.user: Zendesk includes a helpdesk. Do we need a separate one with PARWA?
conversation_flow.jarvis_action: Clarify: PARWA integrates with existing helpdesks rather than replacing them. If they don't have one, PARWA includes basic ticket management. But emphasize that most customers use PARWA alongside their preferred helpdesk, adding AI intelligence on top.

conversation_flow.user: Intercom charges per resolution. What about PARWA?
conversation_flow.jarvis_action: Explain PARWA's unlimited conversation model. Calculate a comparison: if they handle 10,000 monthly resolutions, Intercom Fin would cost approximately $9,900/mo on top of their platform fee, while PARWA Growth at $2,499/mo includes all resolutions.

conversation_flow.user: Can you send us a detailed side-by-side comparison?
conversation_flow.jarvis_action: Confirm and offer to create a customized comparison matrix tailored to their specific requirements (industry, volume, channels, integrations needed). Collect their specific evaluation criteria to make the comparison relevant rather than generic.

key_handling_rules: Never disparage competitors — position differences as different approaches for different needs
Be honest about areas where competitors may be stronger (e.g., Zendesk's helpdesk depth)
Use specific numbers and calculations rather than vague claims
Offer to provide customer references and detailed comparison documents

multi_turn_complexity.title: Technical Integration Assessment for Engineering-Led Evaluation
multi_turn_complexity.scenario: Prospect's engineering team wants to understand technical architecture and integration requirements

conversation_flow.user: What's your API architecture? REST or GraphQL?
conversation_flow.jarvis_action: Explain: RESTful API with JSON payloads, comprehensive Swagger/OpenAPI documentation available at docs.parwa.ai. Webhook support for event-driven integrations. No GraphQL currently but on the product consideration list.

conversation_flow.user: How do we authenticate? Do you support SAML SSO?
conversation_flow.jarvis_action: Detail authentication options: API keys for programmatic access, OAuth 2.0 for delegated access, JWT tokens for session management, SAML 2.0 and OIDC SSO on Growth and High plans. Support for IdP providers: Okta, Azure AD, OneLogin, Google Workspace.

conversation_flow.user: We use a custom-built CRM. How hard is it to integrate?
conversation_flow.jarvis_action: Explain integration options: REST API with full CRUD capabilities, webhook system for real-time event notifications, SDKs available for Node.js, Python, Ruby, and Java, and Zapier/Make integration for no-code connections. Typical custom integration: 2-3 developer days. Offer to schedule a technical architecture call.

conversation_flow.user: What's the data latency between our systems and PARWA?
conversation_flow.jarvis_action: Provide specific numbers: API calls <200ms p99 latency, webhook delivery <500ms with retry queue, knowledge base sync <5 minutes for published changes, real-time event streaming available via dedicated WebSocket connections on High plan.

conversation_flow.user: Can we self-host PARWA or does it have to be cloud?
conversation_flow.jarvis_action: Be transparent: PARWA is currently a SaaS-only platform, no self-hosted option. However, offer alternatives: dedicated infrastructure on High plan provides compute isolation, VPC peering available for network isolation, data residency guarantees, and the security certifications (SOC 2, HIPAA) address most compliance requirements that drive self-hosting requests.

key_handling_rules: Be precise with technical details — engineering teams will verify claims
Acknowledge limitations honestly (no GraphQL, no self-hosting) rather than evading
Offer to connect with technical team for deep-dive questions beyond sales scope
Provide concrete numbers for performance and integration timelines

multi_turn_complexity.title: Multi-Stakeholder Buying Committee with Conflicting Priorities
multi_turn_complexity.scenario: Conversation involves different concerns from CEO (ROI), CTO (security), and Head of Support (ease of use)

conversation_flow.user: Our CEO wants to know the ROI. Our CTO is worried about security. And I (Head of Support) just want something my team will actually use.
conversation_flow.jarvis_action: Address all three stakeholders systematically. Start with CEO's ROI concern since it drives the business case, then address CTO's security requirements, and finally Head of Support's usability needs. Commit to addressing each one fully.

conversation_flow.user: Let's start with ROI. We spend $45K/month on a 20-person support team handling 25K tickets.
conversation_flow.jarvis_action: Calculate specific ROI: 25K tickets × 65% auto-resolution = 16,250 tickets automated. At $12 avg cost per human ticket = $195,000/month savings potential. PARWA High plan at $3,999/month. Net savings: $191,001/month or $2.29M/year. Note that savings come through efficiency gains (team handles remaining 8,750 tickets faster), not necessarily headcount reduction.

conversation_flow.user: That's compelling. Now the CTO wants to know: where does our data go? Who has access?
conversation_flow.jarvis_action: Provide security specifics for the CTO: data stored in customer-selected region (US/EU/APAC), AES-256 encryption at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit, full data isolation between tenants, SOC 2 Type II certified, annual penetration testing, no data sharing between customers, RBAC with SSO/SAML support, complete audit logging. Offer to send the SOC 2 report under NDA.

conversation_flow.user: For me as Head of Support — how much work is this going to add to my plate?
conversation_flow.jarvis_action: Minimize perceived burden: PARWA is managed by our team for setup (10-14 days, customer handles 1-hour kick-off). Ongoing: 15 minutes/week for dashboard review, knowledge base updates only when products/policies change. No prompt engineering, no AI training, no technical management. Provide the team training session (2 hours) to ensure comfort with the platform. Show the agent interface and emphasize that agents give feedback with a single click.

conversation_flow.user: This addresses all our concerns. Can we set up separate demos for each stakeholder?
conversation_flow.jarvis_action: Offer tailored demos: ROI-focused session for CEO with financial modeling, security deep-dive for CTO with technical team, hands-on workflow demo for Head of Support. Provide an evaluation checklist for each stakeholder. Offer to schedule a joint wrap-up session where all three can discuss and align on the decision.

key_handling_rules: Identify and track each stakeholder's priorities separately throughout the conversation
Provide role-specific value propositions rather than one-size-fits-all messaging
Use the organization's own numbers for ROI calculations to maximize credibility
Offer multi-format engagement (demo types, documentation styles) for different stakeholders

language_handling.detection_method: PARWA uses a multi-stage language detection system: First, an ML-based language identifier processes the incoming message to determine the language with 98%+ accuracy. Second, the detected language is confirmed against a confidence threshold. Third, the system checks if the detected language is in the supported list. Fourth, if the language is supported, the conversation is routed to the appropriate language model. If unsupported, the fallback protocol is triggered.
language_handling.language_switching: If a customer switches languages mid-conversation, PARWA adapts within 1-2 messages. The system maintains conversation context across language switches and responds in the language of the most recent message. If the language change is ambiguous, PARWA confirms the preferred language before proceeding.
language_handling.translation_quality: Responses are not simply translated — they are generated natively by the AI in the detected language, preserving brand voice, cultural context, and regional variations. For languages with regional variants (Spanish, Portuguese, Chinese), the system defaults to the most common variant and adapts if the user's dialect becomes apparent.

unsupported_language_protocol.detection: Language identified but not in supported list
unsupported_language_protocol.response: Politely inform the user, offer communication in English or other supported language, provide link to multilingual resources if available
unsupported_language_protocol.fallback: Attempt processing using multilingual model with reduced confidence threshold. If response quality is insufficient, escalate to human agent with a note about the language barrier.
unsupported_language_protocol.admin_alert: Log unsupported language encounters for product prioritization. If a language receives 50+ inquiries per month, flag for potential addition to supported list.

pii_detection.description: PARWA automatically detects and handles Personally Identifiable Information (PII) in customer messages to comply with privacy regulations and protect customer data.

detected_data_types.pattern: Standard email format detection using regex and NLP
detected_data_types.action: Mask in logs, allow in conversation context for support purposes, encrypt in storage

detected_data_types.pattern: International phone number formats including US, EU, and APAC patterns
detected_data_types.action: Mask in logs, allow in conversation context, encrypt in storage

detected_data_types.pattern: Luhn algorithm validation for major card networks (Visa, Mastercard, Amex, Discover)
detected_data_types.action: Immediately redact from conversation, do not store, log security event, warn user not to share payment details in chat

detected_data_types.pattern: US SSN format (XXX-XX-XXXX) and similar national ID formats
detected_data_types.action: Immediately redact from conversation, do not store, log security event

detected_data_types.pattern: NLP-based address detection using street, city, state, zip patterns
detected_data_types.action: Mask in logs, allow in conversation for shipping/support purposes, encrypt in storage

detected_data_types.pattern: Common date formats and contextual detection (DOB keywords)
detected_data_types.action: Mask in logs, allow in conversation for age verification contexts, encrypt in storage

detected_data_types.pattern: NLP detection of medical terms, diagnoses, prescription names, and treatment descriptions (HIPAA contexts only)
detected_data_types.action: Treat as PHI in healthcare deployments, full encryption and audit logging, minimum necessary access principle

data_handling_rules.description: All conversation data is encrypted and stored according to the customer's configured retention policy. Default retention is 90 days. Maximum retention is configurable up to 3 years for regulatory compliance.

PII detected in conversations is automatically stripped before any data is used for model improvement or analytics aggregation. AI training operates on anonymized, PII-free data within each customer's isolated environment.

Credit card numbers are immediately redacted upon detection and never stored in conversation logs. The user is warned not to share payment information via chat and directed to secure payment channels.

When customers export their data (GDPR data portability), all exported files are encrypted with the customer's public key if provided, or standard AES-256 encryption. Export logs are maintained for audit purposes.

When a customer exercises their right to erasure (GDPR Article 17), all PII is permanently deleted from primary storage within 30 days and from backup systems within 90 days. Anonymized analytics data may be retained.

PII is never transferred across regional boundaries unless the customer has explicitly configured cross-region access. Each data region (US, EU, APAC) operates independently with no PII replication between regions.

admin_controls.pii_masking_toggle: Administrators can enable/disable PII masking in conversation logs. Default is enabled. When disabled, PII appears in admin-visible logs but remains encrypted in storage.
admin_controls.custom_pii_patterns: Administrators can define custom PII patterns specific to their industry (e.g., employee IDs, patient MRNs, membership numbers) for additional detection coverage.
admin_controls.data_access_audit: Complete audit trail of who accessed conversation data, when, and for what purpose. Available to Growth and High plan administrators.
admin_controls.automated_compliance_reports: Monthly compliance reports summarizing PII detection events, data access patterns, and retention policy adherence. Available on High plan and Enterprise.
